title: Living Technical Notes on Grid Intelligence
text:
1 Living Technical Notes on Grid Intelligence
These are living graduate-level technical notes on mathematics, control systems, machine learning, power systems, optimization, PyTorch research implementation, and parallel scientific computing.
The notes are developed as part of a long-term study and research program focused on grid intelligence, including power-flow modeling, optimal power flow, learning-based control, scientific machine learning, and scalable simulation workflows.
crumbs: 1  Living Technical Notes on Grid Intelligence

title: Living Technical Notes on Grid Intelligence
text:
1.1 Scope
The notes are organized into six major parts:






NoteStructure




Mathematical Foundations
Proofs, analysis, probability, optimization, dynamical systems, and functional tools.
Control Systems
Linear control, nonlinear control, optimal control, MPC, robustness, and learning-based control.
Machine Learning, Reinforcement Learning, and AI
ML foundations, probabilistic learning, deep learning, RL, scientific ML, and modern LLM systems.
Power Systems, Power Flow, and OPF
AC power flow, distribution modeling, OPF, convex relaxations, DERs, batteries, and hosting capacity.
PyTorch for Research Implementation
Autograd, neural networks, sequence models, transformers, generative models, RL, and scientific ML.
Parallel Computing and Scientific Software Performance
CPU optimization, multiprocessing, CUDA, benchmarking, and accelerated grid analytics.
crumbs: 1  Living Technical Notes on Grid Intelligence

title: Living Technical Notes on Grid Intelligence
text:
1.2 Status
These notes are not a polished textbook. They are evolving technical notes generated from structured study sessions. Over time, selected sections may be reorganized into polished tutorials, dissertation-support material, internal reports, or book-length manuscripts.
crumbs: 1  Living Technical Notes on Grid Intelligence

title: Living Technical Notes on Grid Intelligence
text:
1.3 Intended Use
These notes are meant to support:

rigorous self-study,
PhD research development,
iGridSim-related technical work,
implementation of ML-assisted OPF and grid analytics,
long-term preparation for research in learning, control, optimization, and scientific computing.
crumbs: 1  Living Technical Notes on Grid Intelligence

title: 2  Preface
text:
These notes are meant to support long-term graduate-level study and research development in grid intelligence, optimization, machine learning, control, and scientific computing.
The current version should be viewed as a living technical notebook rather than a finished book. Over time, selected material may be reorganized into polished tutorials, dissertation-support material, internal technical reports, or book-length manuscripts.
crumbs: 2  Preface

title: 3  How to Read These Notes
text:
The notes are organized by major study tracks rather than by final textbook structure.
Each chapter may include:

motivation
definitions
mathematical derivations
algorithms
worked examples
implementation notes
exercises
references

The progress dashboard is included for tracking study and notes-writing progress. Technical chapters should be read as evolving notes.
crumbs: 3  How to Read These Notes

title: 5  Notation
text:
This page collects common notation used throughout the notes.



Symbol
Meaning




\(x\)
State vector


\(u\)
Control input


\(y\)
Output vector


\(A, B, C, D\)
State-space matrices


\(P\)
Real power


\(Q\)
Reactive power


\(S\)
Complex power


\(V\)
Voltage phasor


\(I\)
Current phasor
